title: Three Habits Wealthy Me Already Has
subtitle: Journal prompts for finding the millionaire habits already in your daily life
excerpt: Five journal prompts from Day 56 of a 12-week money program. The prompts look at what daily actions already reflect wealth-building thinking: the saving, the planning, the calm money care that wealthy people practise. The frame is recognition, not aspiration.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 56 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: Five journal prompts for finding the millionaire habits already present in daily life. The practice moves from what daily actions reflect wealth-building thinking, to specific habits that feel calm and expansive, to what daily practice the wealthier version of you would recognise as her own. Set twenty minutes and write without editing.
New to journalling as practice? Read
What daily habits already make me feel wealthy, calm, or expansive?
Not the aspirational answer. The habits that are already in your week.
What does 'living like a millionaire' actually mean to me, not in spending, but in how I think and operate daily?
Write the specific qualities: calm, orderly, intentional, unhurried. What else?
What simple daily actions could help me think and behave even more like the wealthier version of me?
Name them specifically. Actions, not traits.
If I wrote a list of three habits wealthy-me already has, what would they be?
Write the three. Let yourself feel the accuracy of each one.
What is one daily habit I could strengthen this week to close the gap between where I am and where I am heading?
Name the specific habit and the specific time of day.
Adapted from Day 56 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
